recommendations: Time above range is 40.1%. Consider reviewing correction factors and carb counting.

settings_recs.evidence: Consolidated from 2 time-block advisories (range: 13-48%). Overnight quadrant analysis (EXP-2589): BASAL TOO LOW. [CLAMPED from 31% to 25% — stage over multiple adjustment cycles] [Controller: Loop]
settings_recs.rationale: Increase overnight basal by 48% (from 1.70 to 2.52 U/hr). In closed-loop, combining glucose direction with loop compensation direction provides more reliable basal assessment than glucose alone.

settings_recs.evidence: ISF non-linearity (EXP-2511): typical correction dose 2.8U is 60% less effective per unit than 1U (power-law β=0.9). Splitting into 2×1.4U would be ~87% more effective total. [CLAMPED from 60% to 25% — stage over multiple adjustment cycles] [Controller: Loop]
settings_recs.rationale: Correction doses above 1.5U show diminishing returns. At 2.8U, each unit achieves only 16 mg/dL drop vs 40 mg/dL at 1U. Consider: (1) splitting large corrections into smaller doses spaced 30+ min apart, (2) using ISF=16 for doses ≥3U. This is a pharmacokinetic property (β=0.9), not circadian.

settings_recs.evidence: Deconfounded basal block audit (EXP-3447): morning 6:00-12:00 has 4082 clean samples after inferred-meal exclusion, median glucose 152 mg/dL, TBR 1.7%, and Loop actual/scheduled basal median 1.24 with additions >115% in 55% of samples. [Controller: Loop]
settings_recs.rationale: Increase basal by 10% during morning (6:00-12:00). This is a narrow deconfounded step: inferred-meal windows are excluded, Loop is consistently adding basal, and TBR is below 4%. Use the 2-week TBR guardrail before considering another step.

settings_recs.evidence: Correction threshold analysis (EXP-2528): population optimal threshold is 166 mg/dL. Corrections below this level produce net harm (rebound + hypo risk > benefit). Based on 4 correction events. [Controller: Loop]
settings_recs.rationale: Decrease correction threshold from 180 to 166 mg/dL. Corrections below 166 mg/dL show net-negative outcomes: glucose rebounds and hypo risk exceed the glucose-lowering benefit. Per-patient thresholds range 130-290 mg/dL. Predicted TIR improvement: +0.1pp.

recommendations.description: 98% of detected meals have no carb entry. Logging meals improves prediction accuracy and enables better pre-bolus timing.
Time above range is 40.1%. Consider reviewing correction factors and carb counting.

Consider configuring a controller override named "Dinner Aggressive" active 18:00–06:00 with target 100 mg/dL and ISF ratio 0.85 (40 → 34). Late-night peak (292 mg/dL) sits 163 mg/dL above the dinner baseline (129 mg/dL), indicating sustained post-dinner overshoot — current evening settings under-cover the late absorption phase.

Cross-design hypothetical (EXP-2916–2944): a patient with your current profile (TIR 58%, TBR 1.9%, TAR 40%) on Loop migrating to Trio or AAPS (oref1) would expect roughly +14.0 pp TIR (+0.0 pp TBR, -16.3 pp TAR) based on cohort means. This is a directional estimate from cross-design pooling, not a per-patient simulation. Settings tuning on the current controller may capture much of the same benefit (see other recommendations in this report).

correction_energy.interpretation: High correction energy — AID working hard to compensate. Consider settings review. Energy-TIR correlation: r=0.19.

bolus_safety.interpretation: No correction stacking detected. Good bolus spacing (≥3.5h).

aid_compensation.interpretation: High time above range with insufficient insulin delivery. Patient may be genuinely under-insulinized. Consider increasing basal rate or adjusting CR for more aggressive dosing.

optimal_settings.validation_note: ISF appears 1.4× overestimated. ISF is highly stable across time periods.
warnings: CR analysis includes 396 unannounced meal windows (of 404 total) with physics-estimated carbs. Zero-bolus UAM events use circular estimation (true_CR/profile_CR ratio only). Announced meals are preferred when available.

dia_discrepancy.interpretation: IOB decay DIA: 2.7h | Glucose response DIA: 11.9h | Discrepancy: 4.4× — insulin affects glucose much longer than the pump model assumes. | Responder type: fast | Effective ISF ratio: 1.81×

loop_workload.interpretation: Loop workload is very high (100/100). The loop is predominantly INCREASING basal (median ratio: 1.32× scheduled). Basal suspended 23% of the time, increased >150% for 42% of the time.

hypo_risk.recommended_action: No action needed — glucose trajectory is stable.

loop_quality.evidence: 14/55 hypos (25%) preceded by loop insulin increase. median reaction time 25 min before hypo. 131/137 excursions >250 (96%) had no bolus/SMB within ±30 min. Aggression ratio 3.2× (high-glucose vs in-range basal). Overall grade: poor.

dual_phase_isf.paradox_warning: Apparent ISF is NOT true insulin sensitivity — it includes AID controller compensation and EGP suppression. Use demand-phase ISF (0–2h) for conservative recommendations. Multi-factor ISF methods validated: EXP-2640 r=-0.56, EXP-1301 R²=0.805.

warnings: DIA discrepancy: glucose response DIA (11.9h) is 4.4× longer than IOB decay DIA (2.7h).
